title.zh: 大象鬍子藏玄機：從根到尖的「功能梯度」讓象鼻觸覺超靈敏
title.en: The Elephant’s Tactile Superpower: How a Stiff-to-Soft Gradient Makes Touch Sensing Exceptionally Precise

summary.zh:
【總結】大象鬍子從根到尖的「功能梯度」是觸覺超能力！根部70%多孔、堅硬，尖端密實、柔軟，像精密的觸覺琴弦，讓象鼻能精準判斷物體位置。

【研究問題】為何大象不用肌肉控制鬍子卻能精確觸摸？傳統研究 assumptions（均質結構）導致對非主動型觸鬚的機制理解不足。

【研究方法】透過微觀顯微鏡、顯微CT與納米壓痕測試，分析5頭亞洲象不同部位鬍子的幾何形狀、孔隙率與彈性模量，並用有限元模擬驗證。

【結果】根部70%孔隙度+兩倍量級的彈性模量梯度，使觸覺信號強度提升2000%，象鼻能「聽」出接觸點位置，為機器人設計提供生物靈感。
summary.en:
**Summary:** Asian elephants' non-actuated whiskers use a functional gradient in porosity and stiffness to encode contact location through vibrational signals, with root porosity (70%) and stiffness (2.99 GPa) transitioning to dense, soft tips (0.706 GPa).

**Problem:** Why can elephants sense objects without whisker muscles? Prior research assumed uniform whisker properties, ignoring how structural gradients enable tactile perception in non-actuated systems.

**Approach:** Micro-CT and SEM imaging mapped geometry, porosity, and modulus along whiskers; pico-indentation measured stiffness gradients; finite element analysis simulated vibration transmission.

**Results:** Graded whiskers amplified signal power 2000% at 60% length, enabling precise contact localization—inspiring next-gen haptic sensors that balance rigidity and sensitivity.

keyArticle.title: Functional gradients facilitate tactile sensing in elephant whiskers
